title: pinus jaliscana subsp. macvaughii
intro: Pinus jaliscana subsp. macvaughii, also known as the Sierra de Taluca pine, is a subspecies of pine tree native to Mexico. The medicinal properties of Pinus jaliscana subsp. macvaughii include its resin, which has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of this plant include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and other respiratory infections. In herbal preparations, the resin of Pinus jaliscana subsp. macvaughii is often mixed with other herbs to create teas, infusions, and topical ointments to promote healing and reduce inflammation.
